snp4.11:1.1: “From where have there arisen quarrels, disputes,
snp4.11:1.4:
From where have they arisen?
Please tell me.”
snp4.11:2.1: “From what is dear there have arisen quarrels, disputes,
snp4.11:2.4: Tied up with stinginess are quarrels & disputes.
snp4.11:2.5: In the arising of disputes is divisiveness.”
snp4.11:3.1: “Where is the cause of things dear in the world,
snp4.11:3.2: along with the greeds that go about in the world?
snp4.11:3.3: And where is the cause of the hopes & aims
snp4.11:3.4: for the sake of a person’s next life?”
snp4.11:4.1: “Desires are the cause of things dear in the world,
snp4.11:4.2: along with the greeds that go about in the world.
snp4.11:4.3: And here too is the cause of the hopes & aims
snp4.11:4.4: for the sake of a person’s next life.”
snp4.11:5.1: “Now where is the cause of desire in the world?
snp4.11:5.2: And from where have there arisen
snp4.11:5.4: and all the qualities described by the Contemplative?”
snp4.11:6.2: In dependence on that, desire arises.
snp4.11:6.3: Having seen becoming & not- with regard to forms,
snp4.11:6.4: a person gives rise to decisions in the world;
snp4.11:7.2: these qualities, too, when there exists that very pair.
snp4.11:7.3: A person perplexed should train for the path of knowledge,
snp4.11:7.4: for it’s in having known that the Contemplative has spoken of qualities/dhammas.”
snp4.11:8.1: “Where is the cause of appealing & un-?
snp4.11:8.2: When what isn’t do they not exist?
snp4.11:8.3: And whatever is meant by becoming & not- :
snp4.11:8.4: Tell me, Where is their cause?”
snp4.11:9.1: “Contact is the cause of appealing & un-.
snp4.11:9.2: When contact isn’t, they do not exist,
snp4.11:9.3: along with what’s meant by becoming & not- :
snp4.11:9.4: I tell you, from here is their cause.”
snp4.11:10.1: “Now where is the cause of contact in the world,
snp4.11:10.2: and from where have graspings, possessions, arisen?
snp4.11:10.3: When what isn’t does there not exist mine-ness?
snp4.11:10.4: When what has disappeared do contacts not touch?”
snp4.11:11.2: In longing do graspings, possessions have their cause.
snp4.11:11.3: When longing isn’t, mine-ness doesn’t exist.
snp4.11:11.4: When forms have disappeared contacts don’t touch.”
snp4.11:12.4: My heart is set on knowing how they disappear.”
snp4.11:13.1: “One not percipient of perceptions not percipient of aberrant perceptions,
snp4.11:13.2: not unpercipient, nor percipient of what’s disappeared:
snp4.11:13.4: for objectification-classifications have their cause in perception.”
snp4.11:14.1: “What we have asked, you’ve expounded to us.
snp4.11:14.2:
We ask one thing more.
Please tell it.
snp4.11:14.3: Do some of the wise say that just this much is the utmost,
snp4.11:14.4: that purity of spirit is here?
snp4.11:14.5: Or do they say that it’s other than this?”
snp4.11:15.1: “Some of the wise say that just this much is the utmost,
snp4.11:15.2: that purity of spirit is here.
snp4.11:15.3: But some of them, who say they are skilled, say it’s the moment
snp4.11:16.1:
But knowing,
‘Having known, they still are dependent,’
snp4.11:16.3: On knowing them, released, he doesn’t get into disputes,
snp4.11:16.4:
doesn’t meet with becoming & not- :
He’s enlightened.”
